children.text: FAIRY TALE was written by Hiro Mashima, and is part of the shonen genre of anime. The intended audience is young men, but that doesn’t stop younger children or female viewers from watching the anime. The anime has a very diverse cast when it comes to its gender ratio with the show's main narrator being Lucy. Each female character being equally intelligent and powerful and the story giving the female characters backstories room to breathe.FAIRY TALE does a great job at keeping their female characters relevant to the story. Where other popular animes in the genre tend to leave their female characters to the wasteside seemingly forgotten to the story and plot. Seemingly FAIRY TALE seems to be that rare good female representation in shonen anime that is hard to find. (Looking at you JJK and AOT.) The problem with FAIRY TALE is the anime's insistence on having all their female characters nude at some point in time. The fanservice is a consistent aspect of the show that the female characters begin to be dehumanized. Seemingly every battle a female character enters she comes out of it naked. A major complaint of this show is the fan service geared toward the male audience. Implying the only way they can keep their male audience engaged is to have naked female characters. I feel as though it is a valid complaint. The problem with the fan service in FAIRY TALE speaks to a larger problem with anime as a whole: misogyny.
